overview: Written for Parashat Mishpatim from the bride's perspective: the Jewish people in exile, faithful to the covenant, longing to return home. Dedicated to Shmuel Yosef Agnon, Zohar Argov, and to parents, spouses, and future loved ones.
poem_lines: __Under the date tree, beneath the moonlight,__
a bride waits for her long-lost love.
Wine and fruit and flowers await,
But she will not take a bite.
She has learned from Eve's mistake,
Her lips only part for one name.
Her days are filled with sands of exile,
This time, when her groom returns,
**__she will do and she will listen.__**
When will she reclaim her house?
poem_hebrew_lines: אך היא לא תטעם אפילו נגיסה.
